source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/Univariate/PointProcessAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- D6 EMITTER-VERIFIED exact oracle. PointProcessAnalysis drives a Bayesian DEMCzs MCMC over a peaks-over-threshold PointProcessModel (single-GEV mark distribution, explicit threshold/total_years) and assembles the frequency (mode) curve + credible band on the exceedance grid. The values below are the exact output of the real RMC.BestFit PointProcessAnalysis driven by tools/oracle_emitter (BuildAndRunAnalysis mirrors point_process_analysis::run(): drive the seeded BayesianAnalysis serially, then CreateFrequencyAnalysisResults); the seeded DEMCzs chain reproduces the ported C++ core to ~1e-10 relative. Values also reproduce bit-identically across C++/R/Python (shared compiled core + bit-exact Mersenne Twister). thinning_interval pinned to 1. Short run (iterations 100, output_length 400). Tolerance held at rel 1e-5 to absorb cross-platform MCMC float variation (the seeded chain runs many post-guard iterations; libm last-bit differences can accumulate across OSes).
